title: Negate a Vec2
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give a `Vec2` class the two unary operators `operator-` and `operator+`, implemented as `const` member functions. `-vec` returns a vector with both components negated; `+vec` returns an unchanged copy. Neither may modify the original object.

## Input

- One line with two integers `x` and `y` (each between -1000 and 1000).

## Output

- Line 1: the components of `-vec`, space-separated.
- Line 2: the components of `+vec`, space-separated.

Unary operators take no explicit parameters (the operand is `*this`). The provided `main` reads the vector, applies both operators, and prints the results.

title: Logical Not on a Counter
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give a `Counter` class (which wraps a single integer count) the unary operators `operator-` and `operator!`, both as `const` member functions. `-counter` returns a new `Counter` with the negated count; `!counter` returns `true` when the count is zero and `false` otherwise.

## Input

- One integer `n` (-1000 <= n <= 1000).

## Output

- Line 1: the count of `-counter`.
- Line 2: the result of `!counter` as `true`/`false`.

The provided `main` reads the value, applies both operators, and uses `std::boolalpha` for the boolean.
